sessions.intent: Capture an open conversation about PLang's primitive vocabulary — broader scope than this branch, parks here because `number` is one entry in it.

before.assumptions: Ingi opened a design conversation while on the `data-normalize` branch. The trigger was 'we need to define primitive data types in plang' — he wanted TimeSpan, DateTimeOffset (no DateTime), unified number, and 'maybe more'. Pulled in category kinds (image/video/code) after I noted the LLM's vocabulary is the real question. He asked me to file the thinking here for later review without making decisions.
before.risk: If this work happens uncoordinated with the `number-type` plan, the per-primitive OBP shape gets bolted on after `number` ships, leaving `number` as a special case. Counter-risk: widening this branch to a vocabulary overhaul delays `number` and explodes scope. The doc lays out both paths without choosing — that's the user's call.

actions.context: Discussion topic file in the plan/ deep-dive folder. Frames the three-concept collision (wire CLR types / named picks / format kinds), notes that the high-level kind table Ingi remembered is `app/formats/this.cs` (not deleted), confirms the date/time/duration renames he settled, lays out the two-arc vs one-arc fork without deciding.
Added a new top entry summarizing the discussion and pointing at the topic file. Stage table unchanged — nothing in the existing plan moved.

Did NOT modify plan.md or the storage/policy deep-dives. The vocabulary question is broader than `number` itself; mixing it into the spine would suggest it's part of this branch's deliverable when the user explicitly parked it for later review.

after.status: Discussion captured. No decisions made on the broader vocabulary; user to review and decide between one-arc vs two-arc framing.
after.health: Number-type plan is unchanged and still ready to carve. The new doc is informational; nothing depends on it.
after.notes: Conversation happened while on `data-normalize` branch; switched here only to file the writeup. Returning to `data-normalize` after commit + push.

sessions.intent: Design the `number` category type and the arithmetic-policy resolution model.

before.assumptions: Builder handed off a well-scoped task: introduce `number` as a category type to fix LLM-scope drift after `typed-action-returns` (math.* returning Data<object>, decimal literals tagged object). Builder is PLang-side strong but C#-shaky; their `boxed object + Kind` sketch and `int < long < decimal < double` promotion claim were both off and needed re-derivation. Ingi steered: PLang is general-purpose, so decimal-only storage breaks IEEE-754 semantics and isn't defensible; arithmetic policy should be developer-configurable via settings, not architect-imposed.
before.risk: Storage shape (tagged union vs boxed wrapper vs struct) drives allocation cost and convention compliance — wrong call cascades into every math.* handler. Policy resolution (step > goal > app) needs goal-local overlay infrastructure that doesn't exist yet; risk is the overlay leaks across goal boundaries or stays attached during goal pooling. Mixed decimal+double promotion is a real semantic fork — picking wrong gives surprise throws or silent precision loss.

actions.context: Wrote two-layer plan: spine in plan.md (decision narrative, stage index, cross-cutting decisions, verification recipe), deep dives in plan/storage.md (tagged-union storage, arithmetic, parse, equality, ToString) and plan/policy.md (two-axis NumberPolicy, three-scope resolution, environment.number app + goal overlay, environment.set surface). Stages explicitly NOT carved per user request — that's the next session.

Three architectural calls made in conversation, before any file was written: (1) `number` spans all numeric kinds — rejected decimal-only; (2) tagged-union storage with explicit slots — rejected builder's boxed-object pitch and the struct alternative; (3) developer-configurable policy via settings rather than architect-imposed promotion — operators stay policy-free, handlers consult settings.

after.status: Plan written, stages not yet carved (per explicit user instruction). Ready for stage carving in next session.
after.health: Design is settled across the three forks that were live (storage shape, decimal-only vs IEEE-754, policy ownership). One open question logged in plan/policy.md for Stage 2: sub-goal inheritance of goal-scoped overlay (current lean: no inheritance, sub-goal sees App scope).
after.notes: Verification recipe folded from builder handoff. MathHelper.ToDouble / PreserveType in PLang/app/modules/math/MathHelper.cs identified as the deletion target at Stage 3 end — its existence confirms the need for `number`. environment module exists (only `run.cs` today); adding `environment.set` is a clean extension. Goal.Events lazy-overlay pattern is the model for Goal.Environment.
